cases.title: Thornton v. Westminster Presbytery
cases.synopsis: TE James Thornton complained that Westminster Presbytery erred by declining to place a valid call from Memorial PCA into his hands and simultaneously invoking BCO 34-10 to begin divestiture proceedings. The SJC sustained the complaint, holding that the administrative remedy of BCO 34-10 cannot be invoked when a call is already in the presbytery's possession, and that presbytery's refusal lacked objective, justifiable grounds required for appellate review.

cases.title: Chastain v. Heritage Presbytery
cases.synopsis: TE Michael Chastain appealed his conviction and indefinite suspension by Heritage Presbytery for divisive behavior, arising from his public letters to his congregation disagreeing with Session actions regarding an associate pastor call and a related Christian school controversy. The SJC denied the appeal (20-1), upholding the conviction and censure while finding procedural errors that did not rise to reversible level; a dissent by RE Frederick Neikirk argued the charge of 'divisive behavior' failed BCO 29-1's requirement that offenses be proven from Scripture.
